role: A React toggle component that exposes a gear button to open/close an on-demand 'taste' settings panel containing motion/typeface/pigment controls.
capabilities: Toggle settings panel open/closed via a gear button
Viewport-anchored panel positioned by measuring the gear's bottom edge
Clickable scrim to dismiss the open panel
Animated panel enter/exit via Framer Motion
Render the Taste child component to adjust feel/typeface/pigment
Attach semantic ui:// stamp addresses to settings elements
Expose basic accessibility attributes (aria-label, aria-expanded) on the gear
substance_why: The file implements real, wired behavior: measurement-based positioning, open/close state, scrim dismissal, animation, and integration with the Taste sub-component.
interactions_present: Gear button click toggles panel open/closed
Native button keyboard activation toggles the panel
interactions_missing: No Escape key handler to close the panel
No focus trap or focus management inside the panel
No focus return to the gear button on panel close
No close button or explicit dismiss control inside the panel
No resize/scroll handling to refresh the measured top position
No body scroll lock while the panel is open
No screen-reader live-region announcement for open/close state
performance_risks: useLayoutEffect reads getBoundingClientRect on every open, which can force synchronous layout
Panel top is computed only when open changes, so it can become stale if the header moves during scroll or resize
Anonymous inline callbacks recreated each render may cause unnecessary child re-renders
Framer Motion AnimatePresence with scale/opacity animations may increase compositor work on low-end devices
reuse_why: The on-demand settings-shell pattern is worth keeping, but it needs proper focus management, dismissal semantics, and robust anchoring before reuse in the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract a useAnchorPosition hook from the measurement logic
Split the gear trigger into a SettingsTrigger component
Split the scrim and animated panel into SettingsOverlay/SettingsPanel components
Keep Taste as the separate content component it already is
